title: Dark Speculation Deep Dossiers
boundary: Speculation-only layer. These subjects are tracked as motifs, rumours, public-record-adjacent questions, debunk lanes, and open-source leads. They are not presented as fact. No illegal files, no private victim names, no doxxing, no graphic exploitation detail, and no direct accusation against real people without source-specific evidence.
sourceMethod: Separate record, allegation, rumour, symbol, edited media, and court finding.
Every subject needs: origin, claim, evidence status, counter-source, what would change the classification, and safe next route.
Extreme claims require extreme evidence: court record, law enforcement record, authenticated archive, forensic chain, or credible named-source reporting.
If a claim asks for illegal material, the page must refuse the material and track only claim history and public-source discussion.

dossiers.title: Frazzledrip / alleged hidden video claim
dossiers.currentStatus: Track as claim-history and debunk lane only. Do not state the alleged video exists. Do not host, request, describe, or link to illegal material.
dossiers.originPattern: Pizzagate/QAnon-adjacent online rumour cluster tied to hacked-email interpretation, alleged hidden files, and viral forum claims.
dossiers.whyItMatters: It shows how extreme claims spread through secrecy, shock, coded-language interpretation, distrust of institutions, and lack of verifiable source chains.
whatToTrack: how the claim is used to redirect Epstein or trafficking discussions
dossiers.boundary: No illegal files. No private victim names. No direct accusations. Treat as unverified extreme internet lore unless a lawful source-grade record appears.

dossiers.title: Pizzagate / coded network claims
dossiers.currentStatus: Widely debunked as a specific criminal allegation; still useful as a case study in coded-language interpretation, viral network storytelling, and real-world harm from unsourced accusations.
dossiers.originPattern: 2016 hacked-email interpretation, restaurant/social-media claims, and forum amplification.
dossiers.whyItMatters: It teaches the evidence rules: coded words, symbols, photos, and association maps are not proof without source-grade records.
whatToTrack: how similar narratives attach to later Epstein releases
dossiers.boundary: Do not repeat the specific criminal allegation as fact. Classify documents and counter-sources.

dossiers.title: Adrenochrome harvesting motif
dossiers.currentStatus: Track as myth-history, QAnon/Pizzagate-adjacent motif, and moral-panic pattern. Do not present harvesting claims as fact.
dossiers.originPattern: Older drug/occult fiction, Satanic Panic motifs, QAnon-era viral claims, and celebrity/elite rumour loops.
dossiers.whyItMatters: It is one of the darkest viral motifs and can contaminate real trafficking research if not separated from court-record evidence.
whatToTrack: links to older blood-libel style motifs
how it merges with celebrity and trafficking claims
dossiers.boundary: Extreme claim. Track as mythology unless source-grade criminal evidence exists.

dossiers.title: Project Blue Beam / staged sky event claim
dossiers.currentStatus: Classic narrative revived during drone, UAP, hologram, AI-video, and disclosure cycles. Track as scenario speculation, not a confirmed plan.
dossiers.originPattern: Serge Monast-era New World Order claim involving staged alien or religious deception.
dossiers.whyItMatters: It sits at the crossing point of UAP disclosure, AI media fakery, military tech, psychological operations, religious symbolism, and crisis governance.
dossiers.boundary: Track capability and narrative use. Do not claim a staged event is planned without primary records.

dossiers.title: Control-system fusion: ID + money + speech + movement
dossiers.currentStatus: Strongest speculation lane because many components are public-record: digital ID, CBDC research, online safety law, health data systems, border biometrics, climate finance, and AI governance. The open question is integration and lock-in.
dossiers.originPattern: Agenda 2030/global standards arguments, CBDC concerns, digital identity rollout, pandemic pass memories, platform moderation, and security-state expansion.
dossiers.whyItMatters: This is the main bridge between speculation and public-record tracking: individual claims are weak until laws, pilots, contracts, standards, and mandates connect.
dossiers.boundary: Use convergence scoring. Do not claim a finished world-control system unless source-grade records show integration and lock-in.
